channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [AMARSEC] Amarnath Securities Ltd Board Meeting on March 11, 2026: Preferential Share Issue, Auditor Appointment, and Director Changes
item.description:
Amarnath Securities Ltd has announced a Board Meeting scheduled for March 11, 2026. The agenda includes the consideration and approval of a proposal for the issue of equity shares on a preferential basis for cash and/or other than cash. The Company will also seek approval from members for the appointment of M/s. H K Shah & Co., Chartered Accountants as statutory auditors for five years from FY 2025-26 to FY 2029-30. Additionally, there will be changes in the designation of two directors, and an e-voting scrutinizer will be appointed to ensure a fair and transparent process. The trading window for dealing in securities of the Company will be closed for designated persons as per the applicable provisions.

  • Proposal for issue of equity shares on preferential basis for cash and/or other than cash
  • Appointment of M/s. H K Shah & Co., Chartered Accountants as statutory auditors for five years
  • Changes in the designation of two directors
  • Appointment of e-voting scrutinizer
  • Closure of trading window for designated persons

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Amarnath Securities Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [RVHL] Ravinder Heights Ltd. Pays Fine for Alleged Non-Compliance with SEBI Listing Regulations
item.description:
Ravinder Heights Ltd. has paid a fine of Rs. 75,520/- each (inclusive of GST) to the National Stock Exchange of India Limited (NSE) and BSE Limited (BSE) after being allegedly found non-compliant with Regulation 17(1A) of the SEBI Listing Regulations. The non-compliance was for not passing a Special Resolution by the Shareholders of the Company prior to the Appointment of an Independent Director who had attained the age of 75 years. The Company, however, states that it has fully complied with the regulations and the appointment was regularized within the prescribed timeline.

  • Ravinder Heights Ltd. pays fine of Rs. 75,520/- each to NSE and BSE.
  • The fine is for alleged non-compliance with Regulation 17(1A) of the SEBI Listing Regulations.
  • The non-compliance was for not passing a Special Resolution by the Shareholders prior to the appointment of an Independent Director who had attained the age of 75 years.
  • The Company states that it has fully complied with the regulations and the appointment was regularized within the prescribed timeline.
  • The fine was paid on March 05, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ravinder Heights Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [HANNAH] Hannah Joseph Hospital Ltd Authorizes Key Managerial Personnel for Material Disclosures
item.description:
Pursuant to Regulation 30(5) of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, Hannah Joseph Hospital Ltd has authorized certain Key Managerial Personnel to determine the materiality of an event or information and to make disclosures to the stock exchange(s) under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. The authorized personnel include the Managing Director and the Company Secretary and Compliance Officer. Their contact details are available on the company's website: www.hannahjosephhospital.com.

  • Hannah Joseph Hospital Ltd has authorized Key Managerial Personnel for material disclosures
  • The authorized personnel include the Managing Director and the Company Secretary and Compliance Officer
  • The contact details of the authorized persons are available on the company's website

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HANNAH | Category: Business Update

item.title: [AMBIKCO] Ambika Cotton Mills Ltd. Receives Balance 50% Demanded Charges Notice
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Ambika Cotton Mills Ltd. has received a notice from the Superintending Engineer, Dindigul, regarding the balance 50% of deemed demand charges, including interest amounting to Rs. 18007822/-. The notice was received on 05.03.2026, pursuant to the common order dt. 12.12.2025 passed by Hon’ble Division Bench of Madras High Court in WA Nos.947 of 2019. The company has been asked to pay the amount on or before 12.03.2026. However, the company's SLP, filed by Tamilnadu Spinning Mills Association, is listed for hearing on 09.03.2026, and the matter will be dealt with in accordance with the orders of the Hon’ble Supreme Court.

  • Ambika Cotton Mills Ltd. received a notice from the Superintending Engineer, Dindigul, regarding the balance 50% of deemed demand charges, including interest.
  • The notice was received on 05.03.2026, pursuant to the common order dt. 12.12.2025 passed by Hon’ble Division Bench of Madras High Court in WA Nos.947 of 2019.
  • The company has been asked to pay the amount on or before 12.03.2026.
  • The company's SLP, filed by Tamilnadu Spinning Mills Association, is listed for hearing on 09.03.2026.
  • The matter will be dealt with in accordance with the orders of the Hon’ble Supreme Court.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AMBIKA COTTON MILLS LTD. | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [AHCL] Anlon Healthcare Ltd Announces Sub-division of Equity Shares, Alteration of Capital Clause, and Increase in Authorized Share Capital
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Anlon Healthcare Ltd has announced a series of corporate actions in a board meeting held on March 06, 2026. The company has approved the sub-division of equity shares from a face value of Rs.10/- each to Rs.02/- each. This will result in the sub-division of 1 equity share into 5 equity shares. The company has also proposed to alter the capital clause of the Memorandum of Association of the Company. Furthermore, the company has decided to increase the authorized share capital from Rs.55,00,00,000/- to Rs.1,10,00,00,000/-. The company has also announced a bonus issue of shares in the ratio of 1:1.

  • Sub-division of equity shares from Rs.10/- each to Rs.02/- each
  • Alteration of the capital clause of the Memorandum of Association of the Company
  • Increase in the authorized share capital from Rs.55,00,00,000/- to Rs.1,10,00,00,000/-
  • Bonus issue of shares in the ratio of 1:1

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Anlon Healthcare Ltd | Category: Capital Structure Change

item.title: [JDCABLES] JD Cables Ltd Holds Extra-ordinary General Meeting on 6th March 2026
item.description:
JD Cables Ltd, a manufacturer of wires, cables, and conductors, held an Extra-ordinary General Meeting (EOGM) on 6th March 2026. The meeting was conducted through video conferencing/other audio-visual means (OAVM) and the requisite quorum was met. The attendance report, as per NSDL, is attached for reference.

  • JD Cables Ltd held an Extra-ordinary General Meeting on 6th March 2026
  • The meeting was conducted through video conferencing/other audio-visual means (OAVM)
  • The requisite quorum was met
  • The attendance report is attached for reference as per NSDL

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: JD Cables Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [3BFILMS] Resignation of Ms. Urvi Pravin Poriya as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer at 3B Films Ltd
item.description:
Ms. Urvi Pravin Poriya has resigned from her position as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer at 3B Films Ltd, due to personal reasons. Her last working day will be March 10, 2026. She has expressed her gratitude to the Board of Directors and the management for their support during her tenure. She will ensure a smooth handover of responsibilities and completion of all pending compliances.

  • Ms. Urvi Pravin Poriya has resigned from her position as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer at 3B Films Ltd.
  • Her last working day will be March 10, 2026.
  • She has expressed her gratitude to the Board of Directors and the management for their support.
  • She will ensure a smooth handover of responsibilities.
  • She will complete all pending compliances.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: 3B Films Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [MARKOLINES] Markolines Pavement Technologies Ltd. Approves Scheme Of Amalgamation With Markolines Infra Limited
item.description:
Markolines Pavement Technologies Limited ('Company' or 'Transferee Company') held a Board Meeting on 6th March 2026 and approved a scheme of amalgamation with Markolines Infra Limited ('Transferor Company') under Sections 230 to 232 and other applicable provisions of the Companies Act, 2013. The Scheme is subject to the receipt of applicable approvals, including approvals from the respective jurisdictional Hon’ble National Company Law Tribunal, SEBI, Stock Exchange and such other approvals, permissions, and sanctions of regulatory and other authorities as may be necessary. The Scheme will be filed with the Stock Exchange as per the applicable provisions of Regulation 37 of the SEBI Listing Regulations.

  • Markolines Pavement Technologies Limited approved a scheme of amalgamation with Markolines Infra Limited.
  • The Scheme is subject to the receipt of applicable approvals from regulatory authorities.
  • The Scheme will be filed with the Stock Exchange as per the applicable provisions of Regulation 37 of the SEBI Listing Regulations.
  • Markolines Infra Limited's total assets as on 31st December 2025 were Rs. 10,810.09 lakhs and turnover was Rs. 10,411.27 lakhs.
  • Markolines Pavement Technologies Limited's total assets as on 31st December 2025 were Rs. 29,410.75 Lakhs and turnover was Rs. 24,334.19 Lakhs.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Markolines Pavement Technologies Ltd | Category: Merger

item.title: [DCMSIL] DCM Shriram International Ltd Inducts Shri Sunil Behari Mathur as Non-Independent and Non-Executive Director
item.description:
DCM Shriram International Ltd has approved the induction of Shri Sunil Behari Mathur as an Additional Director in the category of Non-Independent and Non-Executive Director with effect from 07.03.2026. Mr. Mathur is a qualified fellow Chartered Accountant and ICWA (Part I & II), London. He has served on the Boards of several reputed companies like ITC, L&T etc., for long periods. He is currently the non-executive Chairman of DCM Shriram Industries Limited and QRG Investments and Holdings Limited.

  • DCM Shriram International Ltd has inducted Shri Sunil Behari Mathur as an Additional Director.
  • Shri Sunil Behari Mathur will serve as a Non-Independent and Non-Executive Director.
  • Mr. Mathur is a qualified fellow Chartered Accountant and ICWA (Part I & II), London.
  • He has served on the Boards of several reputed companies like ITC, L&T etc., for long periods.
  • He is currently the non-executive Chairman of DCM Shriram Industries Limited and QRG Investments and Holdings Limited.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: DCM Shriram International Ltd | Category: New Launch

item.title: [KASHYAP] Kashyap Tele-Medicines Ltd to Review Directors' Performance and Information Flow in March 2026
item.description:
Kashyap Tele-Medicines Ltd has announced that a meeting of the Board of Directors is scheduled on 16th March 2026. The agenda includes a separate meeting of the Independent Directors to review the performance of Non-Independent Directors, the Board as a whole, and the Chairman of the Company. They will also assess the quantity, quality, and timeliness of flow of information. Any other business with the permission of the chair will be discussed.

  • Kashyap Tele-Medicines Ltd Board Meeting scheduled on 16th March 2026
  • Review of performance of Non-Independent Directors and the Board as a whole
  • Review of the performance of the Chairman of the Company
  • Assessment of quantity, quality, and timeliness of flow of information
  • Discussion of any other business with the permission of the chair

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Kashyap Tele-Medicines Ltd | Category: Board Meeting
